content: Just as classroom reflective vocabulary learning moves beyond passive word memorization to build flexible, context-aware lexical understanding by drawing repeated attention to how words connect to new uses, LLM self-reflection can borrow the explicit, iterative 'vocabulary reflection' framework from high school language learning to refine how large language models ground their generation to consistent contextual meaning. This approach frames agentic self-reflection not as an abstract AI-only technique, but as a teachable, iterative skill structured the same way humans learn to reflect on language use.
